# Colorado Individual Income Tax Policy — 2026

## Filing Requirements

Colorado residents and nonresidents with Colorado-source income must file a state income tax return if their gross income exceeds the applicable threshold.

**Filing threshold — single filers:** $14,600
**Filing threshold — married filing jointly:** $29,200
**Filing threshold — head of household:** $21,900

All returns must be filed by **April 15** of the year following the tax year. An automatic six-month extension to October 15 is available; however, the extension applies only to filing, not payment. Any tax owed must still be remitted by April 15 to avoid penalties.

## Tax Rates

Colorado imposes a **flat income tax rate of 4.4%** on Colorado taxable income for tax year 2026. This rate applies to all filing statuses.

Federal withholding adjustments: Employers must apply the **22% federal supplemental withholding rate** to supplemental wages (bonuses, commissions, overtime) unless the employee has submitted a W-4 electing a different rate.

## Standard Deduction and Exemptions

For tax year 2026, the Colorado standard deduction conforms to the federal standard deduction:

- Single filers: **$14,600**
- Married filing jointly: **$29,200**
- Head of household: **$21,900**

Colorado does not allow a separate state standard deduction. Taxpayers who itemize at the federal level must also itemize at the state level.

## Exemptions

Each taxpayer and qualifying dependent is entitled to a **$100 personal exemption credit** applied directly against tax liability (not against income). Qualifying dependents must meet the same definition as under the Internal Revenue Code.

## Penalty for Failure to File

Failure to file a return by the due date (or extended due date) results in a penalty of **5% of the unpaid tax per month**, up to a maximum of **25%**. The minimum penalty for returns more than 60 days late is **$25 or 10% of the tax due**, whichever is greater.

## Penalty for Failure to Pay

Failure to pay tax by the due date results in a **0.5% per month** penalty on the unpaid amount, accruing from the original due date regardless of extensions. This penalty also caps at **25%** of unpaid tax.

## Credits Available for 2026

### Child Tax Credit
Colorado offers a refundable **Child Tax Credit** equal to **25%** of the federal child tax credit amount, available to taxpayers with income below $75,000 (single) or $85,000 (joint). The credit phases out linearly between these thresholds and $95,000 / $105,000 respectively.

### Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
Colorado's EITC is **38%** of the federal EITC for tax year 2026. This credit is fully refundable.

### Property Tax, Rent, Heat Credit (PTC Rebate)
Eligible seniors and disabled individuals may claim a **PTC Rebate** of up to **$1,112** for property tax and rent paid in 2026. Income limit: **$18,026** for single filers, **$24,345** for joint filers.

## Forms

- **DR 0104** — Individual Income Tax Return (primary form)
- **DR 0104AD** — Subtractions from Income Schedule
- **DR 0104CR** — Individual Credit Schedule
- **DR 0104EP** — Estimated Tax Payment Vouchers (four quarterly payments due April 15, June 15, September 15, January 15)

## Estimated Tax Payments

Taxpayers expecting to owe more than **$1,000** in state income tax after withholding and credits must make quarterly estimated payments. Underpayment of estimated taxes results in an interest charge at the **IRS underpayment rate plus 3 percentage points** for Colorado purposes.

## Record Retention

Taxpayers must retain records supporting all items on the return for a minimum of **three years** from the filing date, or **six years** if substantial underreporting (more than 25% of income) is suspected.
